skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: A temperature scale S is defined by the equation S − B = −(4/25)(100 − F), where B is a constant and F is the temperature on a second scale. Which of the following correctly expresses F in terms of S and B?
rationale: Starting from S − B = −(4/25)(100 − F), multiply both sides by 25/4 to obtain (25/4)(S − B) = −(100 − F) = F − 100. Therefore F = (25/4)(S − B) + 100. Choices A and B reverse the sign in front of the (25/4) factor, and choice C uses the reciprocal coefficient.
